description:
Founded in 1991 by Melvin and Rosa John, Kehewin Native Dance Theatre is an Indigenous-led company that shares powerful theatre, dance, and storytelling through international tours, community workshops, and youth training programs. Rooted in cultural revitalization and guided by their unique NAH training process, Kehewin continues to inspire and educate audiences of all ages across the globe.

Join us again this year as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival continues to work with members of Edmonton’s Indigenous Arts Community to present First Nations, Métis and Inuit artists on our stages. We acknowledge that the EFMF takes place on Treaty 6 traditional lands, the home of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux and Metis peoples. We look forward to growing our relationship with Indigenous peoples and communities in the coming years, and we invite you to join us as we walk this journey of reconciliation together. 

Be sure to visit our Nikamowin program of First Nations, Métis and Inuit Exhibits & Installations. Kehewin will be performing from 2:30 pm - 3:00 pm on Saturday August 10 and Sunday August 11 on the Nikamowin stage.
title: Kehewin Native Dance Theatre (Nikamowin)
